subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

questions.q: What is the main difference between s-block and p-block elements?
questions.answer: s-block elements are located on the left side of the periodic table and have one or two electrons in their outermost shell. p-block elements are located on the right side of the periodic table and have three to seven electrons in their outermost shell.

questions.q: What do you mean by periodicity in properties of elements?
questions.answer: Periodicity in properties of elements refers to the recurring patterns and trends observed in the properties of elements as we move across a period or down a group in the periodic table.

questions.q: How do the valency of transition elements differ from that of s-block elements?
questions.answer: Transition elements exhibit variable valency due to the partial filling of their d-subshell. In contrast, s-block elements exhibit fixed valency as their s-orbital can accommodate a maximum of two electrons.

questions.q: What is the main difference between lanthanides and actinides?
questions.answer: Lanthanides are a series of elements with atomic numbers 57 to 71, while actinides are a series of elements with atomic numbers 89 to 103. Both are referred to as inner transition elements.

questions.q: How do the atomic radii of elements vary across a period?
questions.topic: Atomic radii and effective nuclear charge
questions.answer: The atomic radii of elements decrease as we move from left to right across a period due to the increase in effective nuclear charge.

questions.q: What is the significance of the diagonal relationship between elements?
questions.answer: The diagonal relationship between elements exhibits similarities in their chemical properties due to similar ionization energies and electronegativities, despite being in different groups and periods.

questions.q: How do the electronegativities of elements vary across a period?
questions.answer: The electronegativities of elements increase as we move from left to right across a period due to the increase in effective nuclear charge.

questions.q: What is the main difference between isoelectronic species?
questions.answer: Isoelectronic species have the same number of electrons but differ in the number of protons in their atomic nuclei, resulting in different chemical properties.

questions.q: How do the metallic character of elements vary down a group?
questions.answer: The metallic character of elements increases down a group as the number of electrons in the valence shell increases, resulting in a decrease in ionization energy.

questions.q: What is the significance of the periodic table in classifying elements?
questions.answer: The periodic table is a tabular arrangement of elements that exhibits periodic trends and relationships in their properties, making it a powerful tool for classifying and predicting the behavior of elements.
